pi_items.source: Web search + code analysis: Task scripts reference 'American Samoa' consistently (setup_sygic_gps.sh line 114, setup_task.sh line 18). Web search confirmed American Samoa is a real offline map available in Sygic GPS Navigation (https://www.sygic.com/what-is/offline-maps). Task description confirms target: 'Delete the American Samoa offline map'.
Code analysis: setup_task.sh line 22 uses pattern 'find ... -name "*samoa*" -o -name "*as.map*"' to locate American Samoa map files on device. Pattern matches the metadata claim.

Code analysis + task specification: task.json description (line 5) states 'Delete the American Samoa offline map'. verifier.py line 25 verifies 'that the American Samoa map was deleted'. Setup and export scripts track map deletion via file existence checks and storage size changes.

privileged_info_summary: The task uses the real Sygic GPS Navigation app (com.sygic.aura, 154MB APK) to delete the American Samoa offline map region. American Samoa is a confirmed offline map available in Sygic GPS. The map is identified by filename pattern '*samoa*'. The task verification checks for successful deletion via: (1) filesystem checks confirming map files no longer exist, (2) storage usage reduction verification, and (3) VLM-based visual confirmation that the map is no longer listed in the Offline Maps section.
